metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students develop expertise in technical theatre, focusing on lighting design, sound engineering, set construction, costume creation, and stage management. This course emphasizes the creative and technical skills required to support theatrical productions, understanding how design elements contribute to overall artistic impact. Students learn collaborative problem-solving, technical safety, and the important role of production teams in creating complete theatrical experiences.
metadata.general_description: Master technical theatre skills in lighting, sound, set design, costumes, and stage management for theatrical productions.
cross_curricular_connections: Physics and Technology (lighting and sound principles)

big_ideas.statement: Technical theatre elements are essential creative components that contribute to the overall artistic impact of productions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Lighting, sound, set design, and costumes are not merely supportive services but integral artistic choices that shape how audiences experience and interpret theatrical work.

big_ideas.statement: Technical theatre work requires both artistic vision and practical engineering skills.
big_ideas.elaboration: Theatre technicians must understand aesthetic principles, artistic intent, and design concepts while also possessing problem-solving abilities, technical knowledge, and practical construction skills.

big_ideas.statement: Effective technical theatre production depends on clear communication, meticulous planning, and collaborative teamwork.
big_ideas.elaboration: Theatre technical teams must coordinate complex elements, solve problems collectively, document work clearly, and maintain focus on shared artistic goals.

big_ideas.statement: Safety, responsibility, and professionalism are fundamental to technical theatre practice.
big_ideas.elaboration: Theatre technicians work with potentially hazardous equipment and materials; they must prioritize safety, maintain equipment properly, and demonstrate reliability and professional conduct.

competencies.raw: Design and implement lighting systems that support theatrical storytelling
i_can_statements: I can analyze scripts and collaborate with directors to understand lighting needs and artistic intent
I can design lighting plots that support mood, focus, and thematic elements of productions
I can operate lighting equipment and execute cues with precision and artistic sensitivity
I can troubleshoot lighting technical issues and make adjustments for optimal impact

competencies.raw: Design and implement sound systems that enhance theatrical productions
i_can_statements: I can work with directors and sound designers to understand sound design needs
I can record, edit, and mix sound elements for theatrical productions
I can operate sound systems and execute cues reliably during performances
I can integrate live and recorded sound elements effectively within productions

competencies.raw: Design and construct theatrical scenery using appropriate materials and techniques
i_can_statements: I can analyze scripts and collaborate with directors to understand set design needs
I can design sets that support storytelling and create appropriate playing spaces
I can construct scenery using safe techniques and appropriate materials
I can manage scenic transitions and set changes during performances

competencies.raw: Design and create costumes and character-supporting visual elements
i_can_statements: I can analyze scripts and characters to inform costume design choices
I can design or modify costumes that support character development and visual storytelling
I can manage costume logistics and ensure performers have appropriate garments
I can maintain and repair costumes throughout production processes

competencies.raw: Analyze how technical design elements contribute to theatrical meaning and impact
i_can_statements: I can identify how lighting, sound, set, and costume choices support script interpretation
I can explain how design elements create mood, establish place, and enhance storytelling
I can evaluate how technical decisions affect audience understanding and emotional response
I can articulate the relationship between design choices and overall theatrical vision

competencies.raw: Evaluate technical design and execution for effectiveness and efficiency
i_can_statements: I can assess technical designs for clarity, functionality, and artistic impact
I can identify technical successes and areas for improvement in production work
I can problem-solve technical challenges using creative and practical approaches
I can reflect on my technical contributions to overall production success

competencies.raw: Understand the role of technical theatre in supporting performers and serving artistic vision
i_can_statements: I can explain how technical elements support performer safety and performance quality
I can recognize how technical work enables and enhances theatrical storytelling
I can appreciate the artistic contributions of technical theatre team members
I can reflect on my role in supporting the overall theatrical experience

competencies.raw: Develop professional standards and safety consciousness in technical theatre practice
i_can_statements: I can identify and prioritize safety concerns in technical theatre work
I can understand and follow established safety protocols and best practices
I can make responsible decisions about equipment use, construction, and maintenance
I can demonstrate professionalism, reliability, and accountability in technical roles

competencies.raw: Create clear technical documentation and specifications for theatrical work
i_can_statements: I can develop lighting plots, sound cue sheets, and technical diagrams
I can create construction plans and material specifications for scenic elements
I can document costume designs and costume tracking information
I can maintain organized records that support efficient production work

competencies.raw: Communicate technical needs and design concepts with theatrical teams
i_can_statements: I can articulate technical design concepts and their artistic rationale to directors and performers
I can listen to and incorporate feedback from collaborators in technical planning
I can provide clear instructions and guidance to support technical implementation
I can engage in productive problem-solving conversations with team members

competencies.raw: Execute technical responsibilities reliably during rehearsals and performances
i_can_statements: I can perform technical roles with focus, precision, and consistency
I can manage complex technical elements and coordinate timing with performers
I can respond effectively to technical problems during live performance
I can document technical work and share information with team members

competencies.raw: Demonstrate technical expertise and support production teams professionally
i_can_statements: I can train and support other technical team members in developing skills
I can take responsibility for equipment maintenance and organizational systems
I can troubleshoot technical issues and guide colleagues toward solutions
I can present technical work professionally and speak knowledgeably about design choices

competencies.raw: Connect technical theatre elements to principles of design and visual communication
i_can_statements: I can apply colour theory, composition, and design principles to technical work
I can understand how lighting, visual design, and sound work together to create meaning
I can make connections between theatre design and design in other contexts
I can recognize how design choices communicate meaning to audiences

competencies.raw: Explore technical theatre in professional and diverse theatrical contexts
i_can_statements: I can study technical approaches in professional theatre, film, and live events
I can research emerging technologies and techniques in theatrical production
I can explore how different theatrical traditions approach technical design
I can understand the role of technical theatre in diverse performance contexts

competencies.raw: Develop awareness of technical theatre careers and professional pathways
i_can_statements: I can explore career opportunities in lighting design, sound engineering, set design, and stage management
I can understand the training and preparation required for technical theatre professions
I can develop a portfolio of technical work that demonstrates my capabilities
I can engage with mentorship and guidance from technical theatre professionals

competencies.raw: Build comprehensive understanding of collaborative theatre production
i_can_statements: I can understand how all theatre departments work together toward shared goals
I can appreciate the contributions of all theatre team members to production success
I can develop leadership and mentoring skills that support theatre communities
I can commit to ongoing development of technical theatre expertise and professional practice

areas.name: Lighting Design and Technology
topics: Principles of lighting design: mood, visibility, focus, and colour
Types of lighting instruments and their applications in theatre
Lighting control systems and cueing procedures
Script analysis and lighting design development
Integration of lighting with set, costume, and overall production design

areas.name: Sound Design and Engineering
topics: Basic principles of sound: acoustics, frequency, and sound design aesthetics
Audio recording, editing, and mixing for theatrical production
Sound system design, equipment selection, and installation
Sound cueing and operation during rehearsals and performances
Integration of live and recorded sound in theatrical contexts

areas.name: Set Design and Construction
topics: Set design principles and visual storytelling through scenery
Script analysis and collaborative design development
Construction materials, techniques, and safe building practices
Scenic transitions and set changes during performances
Integration of set design with other production elements
Scenery painting, finishing, and maintenance

areas.name: Costumes, Stage Management, and Production Coordination
areas.raw: Costumes, Stage Management, and Production Coordination
topics: Costume design and character support through clothing choices
Costume construction, modification, and maintenance
Stage management responsibilities and production running
Props management and coordination during performances
Production planning, scheduling, and team coordination
Safety protocols and professional standards in theatre production
